title: PTD Today — Media
disclaimer: Informational only — AI-generated summaries; may contain errors. Verify with the original video.

items.title: How to lead and show up every day in your A-game | AWS Events

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Emma Murray is a high-performance mind coach who works with elite athletes, teams, and senior leaders to remove fear and hidden blocks and to cultivate composure, confidence, and presence under pressure. She uses visualization, meditation, theta-state subconscious patterning, and mindfulness, working alongside existing teams via remote and in-person sessions timed to key performance moments.
bullets: Trusted by AFL premiership teams, Formula One, Olympic champions, top executives, and financial traders.
Approach complements traditional psychology and aims to elevate mental performance.
Methods include visualization, meditation, theta-state subconscious patterning, and mindfulness.
Delivers both remote and in-person sessions, scheduled around key performance moments.
Specializes in helping people who falter at critical moments to achieve consistent high performance.
takeaways: Leadership and operational teams can benefit from targeted mental-performance coaching to improve composure and decision-making under pressure.
Coaching that integrates with existing teams and is timed to key events can be applied around high-stakes operations or major rollouts.
Techniques like visualization and mindfulness offer non-technical tools to reduce fear and doubt during critical incidents.
Such programs are complementary to existing psychological support and training, aimed at lifting performance in decisive moments.

items.title: Can New Jersey Survive The World Cup?

ai.summary: Based on the available description, New Jersey and New York will host eight World Cup matches this summer, including the final at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, marking the tournament’s return to the region after 32 years. As the event approaches, New Jersey and FIFA are locked in a dispute over costs, raising questions about whether the financial burden on the state will be worth it.
bullets: The region will host 8 World Cup matches, with the final at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, NJ.
This marks the World Cup's return to the area after a 32-year gap.
New Jersey and FIFA are clashing over who will cover event-related costs.
The cost dispute has sparked concerns about the tournament's financial impact on the state.
The core question is whether hosting the tournament will be financially worthwhile for New Jersey.
takeaways: High-profile cost disputes around major events can affect public budgets—monitor negotiations for potential impacts on permitting, subsidies, or infrastructure funding.
Plan contingencies: allocate flexible budgets and timelines in case state funds are redirected or project approvals are delayed by the dispute.
Engage early with local and state authorities to clarify who will pay for temporary capacity upgrades or infrastructure improvements tied to the event.
Consider proposing scalable, temporary solutions that address short-term demand spikes while offering long-term value to justify investments.

items.title: Cyclops: The Fearless Leader | Character Reveal Trailer | Marvel Rivals

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Cyclops is presented as a disciplined founding X‑Men leader whose ruby quartz visor lets him precisely control devastating concussive optic blasts. He debuts in Marvel Rivals with Season 8.5 on June 12th and the game is free-to-play on Steam and Epic Games.
bullets: Cyclops characterized as husband, leader, and founding X‑Men member.
Uses a ruby quartz visor to control powerful concussive energy from his eyes.
When provoked, he can unleash an unstoppable torrent of concussive blasts.
Arrives in Marvel Rivals with Season 8.5 on June 12th.
Game is free-to-play and available on Steam and Epic Games; multiple social channels listed for updates.
takeaways: Launch date (June 12) is a focal point for potential traffic and patch delivery spikes—ensure CDN and update distribution capacity.
Cross-platform availability (Steam and Epic) can drive higher concurrent user loads—validate load balancing and autoscaling policies.
Marketing across many social channels increases the chance of short-term engagement surges—monitor telemetry and set alert thresholds.
Free-to-play model can lead to sustained growth; plan for ongoing backend resource, storage, and I/O capacity.

items.title: New Way Now: VEED delivers pro-level videos in the blink of AI with Gemini and Vertex AI

ai.summary: Based on the available description, VEED built a high-performance, scalable AI video creation platform on Google Cloud using Compute Engine, Cloud Run, and Vertex AI/Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform. By leveraging Model Garden models like Gemini and Veo, VEED delivers features such as accurate auto-subtitles, multi-language dubbing, a cinematic video generator, and a natural-language AI agent, supporting 12 million users and over a million videos per month.
bullets: Challenge: traditional pro video tools are complex, costly, and inaccessible to most creators.
Built on Google Cloud with Compute Engine, Cloud GPUs, Cloud Run, and Vertex AI to enable performance and automatic scaling.
Model Garden access to Gemini and Veo powers fast iteration of advanced AI features (subtitles, dubbing, cinematic generation, AI editing agent).
Results: 12M+ users, 1M+ videos/month, and 25+ new products launched in the last quarter.
Early support from Google for Startups Cloud Program (credits) helped VEED scale from startup to production powerhouse.
takeaways: Provision GPU-backed Compute Engine instances for low-latency, high-performance video rendering workloads.
Use managed services like Cloud Run to autoscale front-end and orchestration components, reducing operational overhead.
Host, train, and deploy models centrally with Vertex AI to accelerate feature iteration and lifecycle management.
Leverage Model Garden to access cutting-edge generative models (e.g., Gemini, Veo) without building large foundational models in-house.
Startup credits and cloud programs can materially reduce upfront costs and speed time-to-market for resource-intensive media platforms.

items.title: Sztuczna inteligencja w ubezpieczeniach: czy aktuariusze są gotowi na AI? Perspektywa Aon

ai.summary: Based on the available description… The podcast episode explores how actuaries are using advancing technologies, especially AI, in the insurance sector, touching on AI maturity, organizational readiness, and the balance of potential and risk. Hosts Ka Hei Choi (Aon) and Tomasz Dąbkowski (KPMG) discuss productivity gains in modelling, regulatory pressure, responsibility for AI outputs, and infrastructure topics like GPUs and future-proof architectures.
bullets: Overview of technological evolution in actuarial work and current AI adoption.
Discussion of AI maturity, people and organizational readiness.
AI framed as high-potential but risky (Ferrari analogy) and a productivity catalyst for modelling.
Regulatory pressure and human accountability for AI-driven results highlighted.
Infrastructure focus on GPUs and building resilient, future-proof architectures.
takeaways: Anticipate increased demand for GPU compute to support advanced actuarial AI workloads.
Design resilient, scalable architectures to accommodate evolving modelling tools and future needs.
Implement strong transparency, logging and governance capabilities to meet regulatory and accountability requirements.
Ensure secure, auditable data pipelines to support responsibility for AI outputs.
Plan capacity and operational processes to capture productivity gains while managing associated risks.

items.title: Artificial Intelligence in insurance: are actuaries ready for AI? An insight from Aon

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This KPMG ON AIR podcast episode features Tomasz Dąbkowski (KPMG Poland) and Ka Hei Choi (Aon) discussing how AI is reshaping actuarial work, the limits of technology without people and organizational readiness, and what drives successful AI transformation. Topics include AI maturity, productivity gains in modeling, regulatory pressure and human accountability, and infrastructure themes like GPUs and future-proof architecture.
bullets: Explores AI's impact on risk modeling and actuarial work over decades.
Emphasizes that technology alone is insufficient—people and organization readiness matter.
Frames AI as high-potential but high-risk ('like a Ferrari') and a productivity catalyst.
Highlights regulatory pressure and the need for human accountability.
Covers infrastructure topics: GPUs and building a future-proof architecture.
takeaways: Plan for GPU-capable, scalable infrastructure to support AI-driven modeling workloads.
Design architectures that are future-proof and flexible to accommodate evolving AI tools.
Factor regulatory, auditability, and human-accountability requirements into system and data designs.
Prioritize operational readiness—training, governance and cross-functional processes—not just hardware.
Enable productivity gains in modeling by combining compute capacity with appropriate tooling and workflows.

items.title: PoWa™ CRB 1910 Jogging with the FlexPendant

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this ABB Robotics video demonstrates how to jog PoWa™ the CRB 1910 cobot using the FlexPendant and aims to get users up and running quickly. It also directs viewers to free e-learnings and tutorials on the ABB Robotics Online Learning portal and to local, teacher-led training via the ABB Robotics Training portal.
bullets: Shows jogging PoWa™ the CRB 1910 cobot with the FlexPendant.
Designed to help users get operational quickly.
Links to free e-learnings and tutorials on ABB Robotics Online Learning portal.
Offers contact route for local, teacher-led training via ABB Robotics Training portal.
Published by ABB Robotics on 2026-06-09.
takeaways: Available e-learning content can support rapid onboarding of staff to operate the CRB 1910.
FlexPendant jogging demonstrates an operator-level interaction method relevant for technicians.
Free tutorials plus teacher-led training provide flexible training paths to build in-house capability.
Local training center contact offers a clear route to formal, hands-on instruction and support.
